title: taraxacum cornubiense
intro: Taraxacum cornubiense, also known as the Cornish dandelion, is a subspecies of the dandelion plant, native to the southwestern part of England. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly due to its high content of taraxasterol and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The Cornish dandelion has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections, and is also believed to have immunomodulatory and hepatoprotective effects. Herbal preparations of Taraxacum cornubiense can be consumed as teas, infusions, or capsules, and are also used topically in the form of ointments and creams to promote wound healing and skin health.
